ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for kale-fruit-jasmine tea blends specifically. FOODON_03311737 (tea beverage) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers tea and tea-based beverages; used as closest parent as no specific code exists for kale-fruit-jasmine tea blends.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 74242007 (Tea substance) used as closest parent; no specific code for kale-fruit tea blends.

preparation_method: mixed with fresh fruit and vegetables
allergens: none identified (chia seeds may cause reactions in sensitive individuals)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 ml (400 g), equivalent to a large takeaway cup as typically served at HeyTea Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central component foods: Kale raw (FDC ID 323505), Chia seeds (FDC ID 170554), Green grapes (FDC ID 174683), Apples raw (FDC ID 1750340), Lemon juice (FDC ID 167747), Jasmine green tea brewed (FDC ID 171917), Cane sugar (FDC ID 169655); weighted blend estimation for a 400 ml serving with approximately 10 g kale, 5 g chia seeds, 30 g green grapes, 30 g apple, 10 ml lemon juice, 300 ml jasmine green tea, 15 g cane sugar
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Kale Boost Tea exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values estimated by proportional blending of USDA FoodData Central component entries for each ingredient at realistic serving proportions. Per-100g values derived from the 400 g serving estimate. Cane sugar included as default per product description. Values represent a moderate-sugar version; sugar-free variant would have lower carbohydrate and energy values.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fruit-based beverages and green tea blends — analogous estimate based on: Apple juice GI=41 (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care); Green tea GI effectively zero; Sugar-sweetened fruit beverages GI~45–55; blended estimate weighted toward low GI due to whole fruit, fibre from chia, and green tea polyphenols
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kale Boost Tea. GI estimated at 45 based on analogous fruit-tea blends from the University of Sydney GI Database: apple juice GI=41, mixed fruit drinks GI~45-52. The presence of chia seeds (soluble fibre), kale (fibre and polyphenols), and green tea catechins is expected to moderate glycemic response. Cane sugar (default formulation) contributes to GI elevation. GL per serving calculated as (45 × 24.8 g carbohydrate per 400 g serving) / 100 = 11.2.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content; potassium from kale and fruit may support blood pressure regulation
hypertension.guidance: Kale Boost Tea is low in sodium (~20 mg per 400 ml serving) and contains potassium from kale, apple, and grapes, making it generally suitable for individuals with hypertension; however, the cane sugar in the default formulation should be noted, and the sugar-free option is preferable for regular consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added cane sugar in default formulation raises glycemic load; sugar-free option significantly reduces glycemic impact
type2_diabetes.guidance: The default formulation of Kale Boost Tea contains approximately 22 g of added cane sugar per 400 ml serving, resulting in a glycemic load of approximately 11, which is moderate; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should request the no-sugar-added option to substantially reduce glycemic impact and benefit from the fibre and polyphenols in kale, chia seeds, and green tea.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat; antioxidants from kale, green tea, and fruit; omega-3 fatty acids from chia seeds
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kale Boost Tea is very low in saturated fat and provides cardioprotective compounds including green tea catechins, kale flavonoids, vitamin C from lemon and apple, and alpha-linolenic acid from chia seeds; the sugar-free variant is preferred for cardiovascular health to avoid excess added sugar intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kale Boost Tea is a modern fruit-and-vegetable tea blend served at HeyTea in Singapore, combining fresh kale, chia seeds, green grapes, apples, lemon juice, and jasmine green tea. The drink is prepared by blending or mixing fresh and cold-steeped ingredients, resulting in a lightly sweet, mildly grassy, and refreshing beverage with a subtle floral note from the jasmine tea base. The texture is smooth with small chia seed pearls that add a slight gel-like consistency. By default, the drink is sweetened with real cane sugar; a no-sugar-added option is available and recommended for health-conscious consumers. Nutritionally, the beverage provides modest carbohydrates primarily from fruit sugars and added cane sugar, with small amounts of dietary fibre from chia seeds and kale, vitamin C from lemon and apple, vitamin A precursors from kale, and antioxidant polyphenols from green tea and kale. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 45 (low GI range) for the default formulation, with a glycemic load of approximately 11 per 400 ml serving. The drink is low in sodium, fat, and protein, making it a light beverage option. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is halal-friendly. Clinically, the sugar-free variant is preferable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular risk, while the default formulation is moderate in glycemic load and appropriate for healthy adults in moderation. The combination of green tea catechins, kale polyphenols, and chia seed omega-3 fatty acids provides a meaningful antioxidant and anti-inflammatory profile relative to typical sweetened beverages.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Kale Boost Tea exists in any approved primary nutritional database. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional blending of USDA FoodData Central component ingredient entries at realistic serving proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on comparable fruit-tea blends from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component ingredients are individually well-documented in USDA FoodData Central and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component foods; no direct approved-source entry exists for this commercial product. 2. The default formulation includes cane sugar; a sugar-free variant is available and would have materially lower carbohydrate_g (~2.5 g/100g) and energy_kcal (~8 kcal/100g). Values reported here reflect the default cane-sugar formulation. 3. glycemic_index is set to 45 as an analogous estimate based on fruit-tea blends from the University of Sydney GI Database; this is not a directly measured value. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients; naturally zero). 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no vitamin D-containing ingredients; naturally zero). 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — no hydrogenated fats in any ingredient; naturally zero). 7. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — entirely plant-based beverage; naturally zero). 8. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g reported as estimated values from USDA component data. 9. Sentinel value -1 is not used in this record; all fields are either estimated from USDA component data or confirmed zero with justification. 10. Serving size of 400 g used as representative of a standard large takeaway cup at HeyTea Singapore. 11. GL per serving = (GI 45 × carbohydrate per serving 24.8 g) / 100 = 11.2. 12. This product is served at HeyTea Marina Bay Sands, Singapore (field survey listing). 13. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean cuisine category: English (food_name), Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
